name: Beira Litoral Portuguese
notes: Beira-Litoral Portuguese (Aveiro, Vagos), a northern-coastal EP dialect in Cintra's Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirão group (Beira Litoral). MODELLED FEATURES: (1) APICO-ALVEOLAR-ONLY SIBILANT SYSTEM — ⟨s/ss⟩ AND ⟨c/ç/z⟩ all merge to apico-alveolar [s̺]/[z̺] (Cintra 1971 p.93; Álvarez Pérez 2014 pp.37-38, this group 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch'). Coda ⟨s/z⟩ keep chiado [ʃ]/[ʒ]. (2) BETACISM — /v/–/b/ merged to /b/ ~ [β]; Pérez 2014 pp.35-37 shows the bilabial-merger area extends along the coast north of Coimbra (i.e. the Aveiro littoral). A local retroflex lateral [ɱ... ]/[ɭ] realisation of /l/ is reported in regional-dialect literature (not in Cintra/Pérez) and is enumerated as a secondary allophonic variant only. Porto je/wo diphthongisation is not applied. Inherits pt-PT base.

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

sources.author: Cintra, L. F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificação dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.notes: Foundational EP dialect classification. p.93: the Baixo-Minhoto-Duriense-Beirao group reduces the archaic four-sibilant system to two apico-alveolar sibilants [s̺ z̺]; p.88: /v/-/b/ merger and apico-alveolar sibilants are diagnostic northern features.

sources.author: Álvarez Pérez, Xosé Afonso
sources.title: European Portuguese dialectal features: a comparison with Cintra's proposal
sources.notes: ALEPG-corpus quantitative reassessment. pp.37-38: Baixo-Minhotos, Durienses and Beirões 'kept only the apico-alveolar branch' (both s/ss and c/ç/z merge to apical), vs the conservative four-sibilant Transmontano/Alto-Minhoto. pp.35-37: /b/-/v/ merger extends along the coast beyond Coimbra and through the eastern districts (Guarda, Castelo Branco).

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
